ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON SEP-06-2007 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF IS GRANTED PURSUANT TO CCP 36(D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 12/17/07 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206; SETTLEMENT CONFERENCES SET FOR 12/11/07 @ 10:30AM, AND ON 12/12, 12/13, 12/14/07 IN DEPT. 220. PLAINTIFF'S DEMAND DUE BY 12/5/07; DEFENDANTS' COUNTER DEMAND DUE BY 12/7/07. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE ALL PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AND CAUSATION WITNESSES AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 10/5/07, AND SHALL ALSO PRODUCE DOCUMENTS THEY INTEND TO USE AT TRIAL AS TO PRODUCT IDENTIFICATION AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY THAT DATE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. DEFENDANTS TO PROVIDE INFORMATION REGARDING PROPOSITION 51 WITNESSES AND AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES BY 10/26/07. IF DEFENDANTS LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED ON THE DUE DATE. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON, II; CLERK: DLOK; REPORTER: SUSAN LEE CSR #4280 (206)

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 583.310 AND 583.360. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED. DEFENDANT REPRESENTED THAT PLAINTIFF IS IN PROPIA PERSONA. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE PROPER NOTICE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 1005(B), WHICH INCREASES THE 16-COURT-DAY NOTICE PERIOD BY 5 DAYS WHEN SERVICE IS BY MAIL TO A LOCATION WITHIN CALIFORNIA. THE PROOF OF SERVICE DEMONSTRATES THAT DEFENDANT SERVED PLAINTIFF ON DECEMBER 19, 2011 BY OFFICE MAILING TO AN ADDRESS IN CALIFORNIA. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, MATTER NOT REPORTED

Off Calendar Nov-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Eric D. Sentlinger of PERKINS COIE (415-344-7000), appearing for defendant / moving party Honeywell International Inc., F/K/A Alliedsignal, Inc., Successor-in-Interest to the Bendix Corporation

RULING:
DEFENDANT HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL INC., F/K/A ALLIEDSIGNAL, INC., SUCCESSOR-IN-INTEREST TO THE BENDIX CORPORATION'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR FAILURE TO PROSECUTE

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Honeywell International Inc., F/K/A Alliedsignal, Inc., Successor-in-Interest to the Bendix Corporation's motion to dismiss for failure to prosecute pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310, 583.360 is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Foster Wheeler LLC's motion to dismiss pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310 is granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez, CSR #12436 (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Delay In Prosecution
Off Calendar Mar-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR CO.'S MOTION TO DISMISS FOR DELAY IN PROSECUTION PURSUANT TO CCP 583.310 AND 583.360.
Appearances: J. Paul Fanning of Lankford Crawford Moreno LLP (925.300.3520) for defendant and moving party; no other appearances.
The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. No opposition filed. Order signed. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

Notice Of Motion To Dismiss Action For Delay In Prosecution
Off Calendar Jan-19-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Ford Motor Company's motion to dismiss for delay in prosecution pursuant to C.C.P. 583.310 and 583.360 is denied. Defendant represented that plaintiff is in propia persona. Defendant failed to provide proper notice pursuant to C.C.P. 1005(b), which increases the 16-court-day notice period by 5 days when service is by mail to a location within California. The proof of service demonstrates that defendant served plaintiff on December 19, 2011 by office mailing to an address in California. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)
